response.justification: The candidate has a long and varied software career with genuine breadth across web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure domains, but fundamentally does not meet the minimum qualifications for this role. The job's primary hard requirement is 8 years of C++ experience — C++ appears only in one historical role (NI LabWindows components) and a bachelor thesis project, and is conspicuously absent from the candidate's skills section. The candidate's demonstrated expertise is concentrated in TypeScript/Node.js/DLang web and product engineering, not in low-level systems, cloud compute platforms, or C++-based infrastructure. Technical leadership experience exists but is informal (workshops, open source, code dojos) rather than the structured cloud/DevOps team mentoring the role demands.
strengths: Proven exposure to high-throughput distributed systems: micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests per second using async I/O and high-performance databases
Strong knowledge-sharing and mentoring culture demonstrated through recurring code katas, public workshops, conference talks, and open-source library authorship
Broad infrastructure awareness: Kubernetes deployments, AWS CDK, Docker, GitLab CI/CD, and DevOps tooling across multiple roles
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and dated — limited to one historical role and a thesis project; C++ does not appear in the skills section, nowhere near the 8-year minimum requirement
Core technical stack is web-oriented (TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB), which is misaligned with the cloud compute platform, systems programming, and hardware architecture focus of this role
No evidence of production-level cloud architecture ownership, platform engineering, or technical leadership over cloud/infrastructure teams at the scale and depth the role requires

key_factors.factor: Web/product engineering focus vs. systems/infrastructure role
